relevant_files.role: EXTENSION_MAP constant and language_for_extension() function; pattern for registering .kt and .kts file extensions
get_language_info() match statement with all language arms; get_ts_language() function; LanguageInfo struct definition with all fields
Reference implementation: ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, DEFUSE_QUERY constants; extract_inheritance() handler; test patterns
Feature flags definition pattern; optional dependencies declaration; default feature set
Workspace dependencies section; tree-sitter and language-specific crate versions
Test module structure: parse_java() helper, test_element_query, test_extract_inheritance, test_defuse_query patterns

conventions.commits: Conventional Commits with GPG signature (-S) and DCO sign-off (--signoff); types: feat, fix, docs, refactor, test, chore; scope in parentheses (e.g., feat(lang): add Kotlin support)
conventions.testing: Unit tests in module under #[cfg(all(test, feature = "lang-X"))]; AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert); parse_<lang>() helper using tree_sitter::Parser; test element_query, call_query, reference_query, import_query, extract_inheritance, defuse_query
conventions.linting: cargo fmt --check for formatting; cargo clippy -- -D warnings for lint; cargo deny check advisories licenses for dependencies
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for custom error types; Option<T> for fallible lookups; Result<T, E> for parser operations; None for unsupported languages
conventions.imports: tree_sitter crate for Language, Node, Parser, Query, QueryCursor; language-specific tree_sitter_<lang> crates imported conditionally via feature gates

patterns: Feature-gated language modules: #[cfg(feature = "lang-X")] pub mod x; paired with optional dependency in Cargo.toml [features] section
EXTENSION_MAP constant: tuple array of (&str, &str) with #[cfg(feature = "lang-X")] guard on each entry mapping extension to language name
LanguageInfo struct: name, language (via tree_sitter_<lang>::LANGUAGE), element_query, call_query, reference_query, import_query, defuse_query, and optional handler functions
Query constants as &str: tree-sitter S-expression syntax; ELEMENT_QUERY captures @function, @class, @interface; CALL_QUERY captures @call; DEFUSE_QUERY captures @read, @write, @writeread
extract_inheritance() function: walks delegation_specifiers or superclass/interfaces fields; returns Vec<String> with 'extends X' or 'implements Y' format; None handlers for Kotlin (no impl blocks)
Test module: #[cfg(all(test, feature = "lang-X"))]; parse_<lang>() helper; Query::new() to validate query syntax; QueryCursor::matches() to execute; assertions on captured names and node content

related_issues.title: feat(lang): add Swift grammar support
related_issues.relevance: Similar language addition task; may inform Kotlin implementation patterns

related_issues.title: bench(go): v16 Go interface dispatch analysis benchmark
related_issues.relevance: Related to language feature analysis; Go has similar interface patterns

related_issues.title: bench(analyze_symbol): v15 MCP-only def-use micro-benchmark
related_issues.relevance: Tests def-use analysis which Kotlin DEFUSE_QUERY must support

constraints: Kotlin is JVM-first with class-based OOP; inheritance via delegation_specifiers (superclass with constructor_invocation parens, interfaces as user_type without parens)
No impl blocks in Kotlin; interface implementation is inline with delegation_specifiers; impl_query and impl_trait_query must be None
Companion objects are static-like members inside classes; declared as 'companion object { ... }' inside class; no special extraction needed for symbol analysis
tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 is the required version; must be added to workspace.dependencies in root Cargo.toml
Feature flag lang-kotlin must be added to default feature set in crates/code-analyze-core/Cargo.toml; tree-sitter-kotlin must be optional dependency
Extensions .kt and .kts must be registered in EXTENSION_MAP with feature gate; supported_languages() must include 'kotlin'
No extract_function_name, find_method_for_receiver, find_receiver_type handlers needed for Kotlin (set to None in LanguageInfo)
test_coverage: Existing test coverage for language registration: test_language_for_extension_happy_path and test_language_for_extension_edge_case in lang.rs verify extension lookup; test_get_language_info_known/unknown and test_get_ts_language_known/unknown in languages/mod.rs verify language info retrieval. New kotlin.rs tests must cover: element_query (function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration with data/companion modifiers), call_query (call_expression), reference_query (identifier references), import_query (import_header), extract_inheritance (single superclass with constructor_invocation, multiple interfaces as user_type, mixed inheritance and interface delegation)

library_findings.relevant_api: tree_sitter_kotlin::LANGUAGE constant; node kinds: function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration, delegation_specifier, constructor_invocation, user_type, call_expression, import_header
library_findings.notes: Source: fwcd/tree-sitter-kotlin (High reputation, 79.1 benchmark score, 576 code snippets). Kotlin AST uses delegation_specifiers field on class_declaration; each delegation_specifier is either constructor_invocation (superclass with parens) or user_type (interface, no parens). function_declaration has simple_identifier for name. import_header contains identifier nodes. No separate impl blocks; inheritance is inline.

library_findings.relevant_api: Language, Node, Parser, Query, QueryCursor, StreamingIterator; Parser::set_language(), Parser::parse(); Query::new(); QueryCursor::matches(); Node::child_by_field_name(), Node::named_child(), Node::kind(), Node::start_byte(), Node::end_byte()
library_findings.notes: Core tree-sitter Rust bindings. Used for parsing, querying, and traversing AST. All language modules depend on this.

approaches.name: Approach 1: Minimal Kotlin with DEFUSE_QUERY (Recommended)
approaches.description: Add kotlin.rs with ELEMENT_QUERY (function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration), CALL_QUERY (call_expression), REFERENCE_QUERY (identifier), IMPORT_QUERY (import_header), and DEFUSE_QUERY (variable declarations, assignments, identifier reads). Implement extract_inheritance() to walk delegation_specifiers and separate superclass (constructor_invocation with parens) from interfaces (user_type, no parens). Register .kt and .kts extensions in EXTENSION_MAP. Add lang-kotlin feature to Cargo.toml with tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 dependency. Add kotlin arm in get_language_info() and get_ts_language() with all queries populated and handler functions set to None. Create unit tests covering all element types, calls, imports, and inheritance patterns.
pros: Provides complete def-use analysis for Kotlin via DEFUSE_QUERY; enables symbol tracking and call graph analysis
Follows established pattern from Java (similar JVM-based language with class hierarchy and imports)
Minimal handler functions (all None) reduces complexity and maintenance burden
Comprehensive test coverage ensures correctness and prevents regressions
Aligns with issue acceptance criteria and maintainer guidance
cons: DEFUSE_QUERY requires careful tuning to Kotlin's variable declaration and assignment syntax; may need iteration
Larger initial implementation (~200-250 lines for kotlin.rs); more test cases to maintain
Kotlin-specific syntax (val/var, data class, companion object) requires accurate node kind mapping

approaches.name: Approach 2: Core Kotlin without DEFUSE_QUERY (Justifiable Omission)
approaches.description: Add kotlin.rs with ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, and IMPORT_QUERY only; set DEFUSE_QUERY to None. Implement extract_inheritance() as in Approach 1. Register extensions and feature flag identically. Add kotlin arm in get_language_info() with defuse_query: None. Justification: Kotlin's variable scoping and assignment patterns differ from Java (val/var immutability, destructuring, lambda captures); DEFUSE_QUERY would require extensive testing and validation. Symbol extraction (functions, classes, calls, imports) is sufficient for code structure analysis without def-use tracking.
pros: Faster initial implementation (~150 lines for kotlin.rs); fewer test cases
Reduces risk of incorrect DEFUSE_QUERY matching Kotlin-specific syntax
Still provides core symbol extraction (functions, classes, calls, imports) for MCP tools
Easier to add DEFUSE_QUERY later once Kotlin semantics are better understood
Acceptable trade-off if def-use analysis is not critical for Kotlin use cases
cons: Incomplete feature set compared to Java; users cannot track variable definitions and uses
Does not fully meet issue acceptance criteria (DEFUSE_QUERY is listed in requirements)
Limits code-analyze-mcp's value for Kotlin-based projects requiring def-use analysis
Inconsistent with other languages (all have DEFUSE_QUERY except Fortran)

approaches.name: Approach 3: Extended Kotlin with Handler Functions
approaches.description: Add kotlin.rs with all queries (ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, DEFUSE_QUERY) and implement extract_function_name() and extract_inheritance() handlers. extract_function_name() extracts function name from function_declaration node via simple_identifier field. extract_inheritance() walks delegation_specifiers. Set find_method_for_receiver and find_receiver_type to None (Kotlin does not have receiver type inference patterns like Go). Register extensions and feature flag. Add kotlin arm in get_language_info() with handler functions populated. Create comprehensive tests.
pros: Provides maximum semantic information for Kotlin code analysis
extract_function_name() enables precise function identification in call graphs
Future-proofs for potential receiver type analysis if Kotlin extension function patterns are added
Consistent with languages that have handler functions (Rust, C++, Go)
cons: Larger implementation (~250-300 lines); more handler code to test and maintain
extract_function_name() may be redundant if ELEMENT_QUERY already captures function names accurately
Kotlin extension functions (receiver type) are less common than in Rust; handler may not be used
Adds complexity without clear benefit over Approach 1 for typical Kotlin analysis tasks

recommendation: Approach 1 (Minimal Kotlin with DEFUSE_QUERY). This approach balances completeness, maintainability, and alignment with issue requirements. It provides full symbol extraction and def-use analysis (critical for code structure tools), follows the established Java pattern (both JVM-based languages with similar class hierarchies), and requires no custom handler functions (reducing complexity). The DEFUSE_QUERY can be validated through comprehensive unit tests covering Kotlin's val/var declarations, assignments, and identifier references. This approach satisfies all acceptance criteria in issue #649 and positions code-analyze-mcp for comprehensive Kotlin code analysis across MCP tools.
